hints.text: Plates **black w/ white lettering + yellow-and-red emblem** near the left — blurred they look grey with the emblem colours still visible
hints.uniq_note: In Europe only Liechtenstein has this black plate w/ yellow-red emblem; Swiss cars (white plates) are constantly around though.

hints.text: Most coverage in a **flat river valley w/ tall mountains in almost every direction** — nearby mountains almost always towering to the **east**; lots of pastures/farmland (Rhine valley)
All coverage = **low-cam**: significantly bigger car blur, roads look wider, low viewing angle

shared_with.diff: Black plates + LIEMOBIL bus stops → Liechtenstein.

shared_with.note: Japan is the only other always-low-cam country.

hints.text: Bollards **black-and-white cylindrical, rounded top**, white/grey reflector, often a **blue side attachment**; on guardrails: wedge design instead

shared_with.note: Swiss bollards can be almost identical.

hints.text: Bus stops: **green-and-white design w/ 'LIEMOBIL'** at the top, black metallic border (frame not always black)
Directional signs white (smaller roads) or blue (bigger roads) w/ **pointed contrasting arrowhead**
Pedestrian signs: **7 stripes in an oddly tall rectangle**
**Yellow-black striped road separators**: rounded top w/ a Lego-like knob, 3 stripes each colour, yellow at the bottom
Road signs commonly in a **round grey metal border**

shared_with.note: Swiss signs have similar metallic borders.

hints.text: Architecture: blend of **traditional Alpine + modern buildings** (modern esp. in the bigger towns)

bullets: Triesenberg: densely populated mountain slope east of the valley, Rhine valley visible below to the west
East of the central ridge: mountains all around
Ruggell: only a small forested ridge to the south (big contrast to elsewhere)
Mauren: ridge to the north (urban side) + big mountains south/southeast
Bendern → Austrian border: rolling hills (rest of FL is flat or alpine)
West of Balzers: flat, NE-gridded roads; two rocky peaks on the Swiss side visible N/NW
Vaduz: sometimes RED bus-stop signs; brown street signs w/ traditional German-style font (also northern towns)
South + around Bendern: blue street signs w/ thin white border
Eschen: blue street signs WITHOUT outline
Schaan: black signs w/ white border + embossed letters
Ruggell: brown arrow-design signs (unique) + regular brown
Planken: brown signs w/ generic font

hints.text: **Spotlights**: steep rocky cliff w/ trees at base & top → in/around Mäls; the only lakes → just south of Steg (middle of the mountains)
